text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Fifteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time July 12 2026 This weekend July 11th 12th the Church celebrates the Fifteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time Our readings are clearly united by the motif of sowing the seed of God's Word The Christian is conformed to the seed of the Word Just as Christ the Word came to earth and was planted in the ground through death but rose as the first fruits on the third day so the Christian too will finally be planted but will also rise again This applies not only to our hope in the resurrection of the body but also in a spiritual sense to our daily lives as Christians Our daily lives often involve countless contradictions frustrations humiliations sufferings and even persecutions We accept all of these little deaths throughout the day in order that we might become that soil tilled and broken open to receive the seed so that it might bear fruit a hundred sixty or thirtyfold through our lives Special Collections Mission Collection Venezuela This weekend we welcome Sr Miriam Therese of the Daughters of Divine Love The Daughters of Divine Love were founded on July 16 1969 in Nigeria partially in response to the brutal Nigeria Biafra War as a witness to Gods love throughout the world Today their community has over 1200 sisters most notably recognized by the signature blue veil worn as a part of their religious habit ministering in 15 countries around the world We thank Sr Miriam Therese for sharing a bit about her community with us and the missionary work to which they have been called As a reminder we have the opportunity to help financially support their missionary efforts with our special mission collection Donations can be placed in the mission basket in the vestibule next to our regular offertory basket throughout this weekend In addition to our mission collection Bishop Rhoades has requested that each parish throughout our diocese take up a special collection this weekend to assist with the relief efforts in Venezuela following the deadly earthquakes at the end of June This collection will be sent to Catholic Relief Services to support the work of both Caritas Venezuela and local parishes in Venezuela providing critical relief including shelter food and other humanitarian aid As we had already scheduled our mission collection and speaker for this weekend several months ago we will be supporting this special collection for Venezuela by making a large donation on behalf of our parish through our St Vincent de Paul Fund This allows us to support those in immediate need from this tragedy without affecting our parish's financial generosity to the Daughters of Divine Love and the good work that they are doing Sanctuary Flooring and Liturgical Arrangements If you were with us last weekend you had the chance to enjoy my short lesson on liturgical arrangements at the beginning of Mass As I had mentioned we received our long awaited flooring tiles for the sanctuary just before the holiday weekend The tiles arrived on several pallets each weighing half a ton Several of these pallets have been stored behind the dust screen in preparation for the start of the flooring work beginning this coming Monday Since mid April we have been opening a portion of the dust screen each weekend to expand our make shift sanctuary back into the construction zone Doing so has allowed us to make use of the liturgical altar style known as the versus populum style With the storage of the tile and needed preparation of the flooring behind the dust screen however we are no longer able to safely expand into the construction area until the tiling is completed Without this additional space that we would normally gain from opening the dust screen we have switched from using the versus populum style to using another liturgical altar option the ad orientem style This stylistic change allows us to greatly shrink the needed footprint for our make shift sanctuary and ensures that there is a safe amount of space between the altar and front pew to help facilitate the distribution of Holy Communion Saturday July 11 400 4:45pm Confession 5:00pm Julayne Miller Sunday July 12 7:10am Rosary 7:30am Dale Morrissey congregation raised 257207 to help provide hope resources and practical support to the individuals and families we serve in our community We are truly grateful for your generosity support and prayers Kelli Kistler Advancement Director Help Hands Sanctuary Flooring and Liturgical Arrangements Cont As I often say when it comes to naming the Church is not very creative Both of these altar styles are described by their names The versus populum style means towards the people In this style as we enter into the Liturgy of the Eucharist the priest goes to the back of the altar so that he is facing towards the congregation This allows the congregation a better physical view of what is occurring on the altar throughout the Eucharistic Prayer The ad orientem style means to the east In this style as we enter the Liturgy of the Eucharist the priest goes to the front of the altar so that he is facing with the congregation In this way we face the same direction together in prayer This physical posture reminds the congregation that throughout the Eucharistic Prayer we are turned together as one towards the Father united as one spiritual body in offering the one Sacrifice of Christ It is important to remember that neither altar style changes the Mass itself Christ becomes just as truly present upon the altar and we are made present to the same Sacrifice of Calvary Each style simply emphasizes a different aspect of the same mystery Regardless of which style is used our attention always remains fixed on Jesus Christ who gathers us together and offers Himself to the Father for our salvation Faithfully In Christ Fr David Knights of Columbus Father Aloysius Phillips Council 7555 116 Dustman Rd Bluffton (260)824-4263 Open Monday through Thursday 9am 5pm Serving All Surrounding
